_comment: Fairfax County Police Officers Retirement System (PORS). Fully independent defined benefit pension system (not VRS). Established 1944 (oldest of Fairfax County's three retirement systems). Covers sworn full-time law enforcement officers of the Fairfax County Police Department only. Three plan tiers (A-C) based on hire date. Update as Fairfax County Code changes.
systemName: Fairfax County Police Officers Retirement System
employer: Fairfax County Police Officers Retirement System

jurisdiction.note: PORS is a fully independent single-employer defined benefit pension system established 1944 under Chapter 303 of the Acts of Assembly. NOT part of VRS. In 1981 Fairfax County enacted Article 7 of the Fairfax County Code adopting and continuing Chapter 303. Covers only sworn full-time police officers of the Fairfax County Police Department. Governed by Fairfax County Code Chapter 3, Article 7.

hireDateMapping.label: PORS Plan A (pre-2013)
hireDateMapping.note: Sworn officers hired (sworn in) before 1/1/2013. CLOSED to new members.

hireDateMapping.label: PORS Plan B (2013-2019)
hireDateMapping.note: Sworn officers hired (sworn in) 1/1/2013 through 6/30/2019. CLOSED to new members.

hireDateMapping.label: PORS Plan C (2019+)
hireDateMapping.note: Sworn officers hired (sworn in) on or after 7/1/2019. OPEN to new members.

pors_plan_a.statusNote: Closed to new members as of 1/1/2013. Existing members retain Plan A provisions.
pors_plan_a.eligibleEmployees: Sworn full-time police officers of the Fairfax County Police Department hired (sworn in) before 1/1/2013.

finalAverageSalary.note: Average of highest 78 consecutive biweekly pay periods (36 months). FMLA/LWOP periods are excluded from FAS calculation. Military leave compensation is imputed if member was unable to use paid leave.

benefitFormula.maxServiceNote: No statutory cap on benefit service years in PORS formula.

normalRetirement.note: Age 55 OR 25 years of creditable service (including sick leave credit).

earlyRetirement.condition: Under age 55 with 20+ years of creditable service. Benefit reduced based on both age and service.
earlyRetirement.reductionNote: Normal retirement formula applies with a reduction factor based on age and years of service at early retirement.

deferredVested.formula: Normal retirement formula applied to service at termination
deferredVested.note: Must leave contributions in the System. Deferred annuity payable at age 55.

nonServiceConnected.serviceRequirement: At least 5 years of creditable service
nonServiceConnected.medicalReExam: Annually for first 5 years, then every 3 years
nonServiceConnected.note: Total disability only; no partial non-service-connected disability. Benefits are taxable. No automatic surviving spouse/children benefit payable.

serviceConnected.formula: 66.67% of salary at time of disability
serviceConnected.note: 66⅔% of salary at time of disability less Workers' Compensation benefits.

cola.formula: Lesser of 4.0% or Washington-Arlington-Alexandria CPI-U increase for 12 months ending March 31
cola.adHocCola: Additional 1.0% ad-hoc COLA at Board of Trustees discretion when system funding ratio exceeds 100%
cola.note: CPI-U is the Consumer Price Index for All Urban Consumers for the Washington-Arlington-Alexandria, DC-VA-MD-WV area. First-year retirees receive prorated COLA.

drop.eligibility: Must be eligible for normal retirement (age 55 or 25 years of service)
drop.interestNote: Pro-rated annual rate of 5% credited to DROP account
drop.note: Election irrevocable after effective date. Employee remains active for all other purposes during DROP. Sick leave must be converted to retirement credit at DROP entry (option to retain 40 hours).

survivorOptions.automaticDeathBenefitNote: Automatic death benefit payable to eligible surviving spouse and children upon death before retirement, after service retirement, or after service-connected disability retirement. Not payable if death occurs while receiving non-service-connected or deferred vested benefit.
survivorOptions.preRetirementDeath: Return of employee contributions to beneficiary unless automatic benefit payable to surviving spouse/children

sickLeaveCredit.conversionRate: 172 hours equals one month of creditable service

employeeContribution.note: 8.65% of retirement-eligible gross earnings. Rate reduced from 12% (pre-2007) to 11% (2007), 10% (2008), then 8.65% (2014). Rate change does not affect benefit calculations.
employeeContribution.socialSecurityNote: PORS members do NOT participate in Social Security.

membershipStats.note: As of June 30, 2023 per Fairfax County ACFR.

pors_plan_b.statusNote: Closed to new members as of 7/1/2019. Existing members retain Plan B provisions.
pors_plan_b.eligibleEmployees: Sworn full-time police officers of the Fairfax County Police Department hired (sworn in) 1/1/2013 through 6/30/2019.

Not payable if death occurs while receiving non-service-connected or deferred vested benefit.
survivorOptions.preRetirementDeath: Return of employee contributions to beneficiary unless automatic benefit payable to surviving spouse/children

sickLeaveCredit.conversionRate: 172 hours equals one month of creditable service
sickLeaveCredit.maxHoursNote: For officers hired on or after 1/1/2013, maximum accrued sick leave credit for retirement eligibility and benefit computation is the employee's accrued sick leave balance, with credit capped at 2,080 hours.

employeeContribution.note: 8.65% of retirement-eligible gross earnings. Rate reduced from 12% (pre-2007) to 11% (2007), 10% (2008), then 8.65% (2014). Rate change does not affect benefit calculations.
employeeContribution.socialSecurityNote: PORS members do NOT participate in Social Security.

membershipStats.note: As of June 30, 2023 per Fairfax County ACFR.

pors_plan_c.statusNote: OPEN to new members. All sworn officers hired on or after 7/1/2019 are automatically enrolled in Plan C.
pors_plan_c.eligibleEmployees: Sworn full-time police officers of the Fairfax County Police Department hired (sworn in) on or after 7/1/2019.

finalAverageSalary.note: Average of highest 78 consecutive biweekly pay periods (36 months). FMLA/LWOP periods are excluded from FAS calculation. Military leave compensation is imputed if member was unable to use paid leave.

benefitFormula.escalatorNote: No 3% escalator for Plan C. This is the primary difference from Plans A and B.
benefitFormula.maxServiceNote: No statutory cap on benefit service years in PORS formula.
